results.rendered_html: gayā f. (g. varaṇādi) the city Gayā (famous place of pilgrimage in Behar and residence of the saint Gaya; cf.  RTL. p. 309 ; sanctified by Viṣṇu as a tribute to the piety of Gaya, the Rājarṣi, or (according to another legend) to Gaya, the Asura, who was overwhelmed here with rocks by the gods; the Śrāddha should be performed once at least in the life of every Hindū to his progenitors at Gayā),  Yājñ. i, 260 ;  MBh.  &c.
